_doc: Per-file byte budgets for the ALWAYS-ON context stack (loaded every turn via the system prompt + CLAUDE.md @-imports). BudgetCheck.ts fails when a file exceeds its budget — backpressure against re-accretion (the disease the doctrine-prune treats). Budgets are ceilings, not targets: to add, first prune. Tight budgets on the re-bloat offenders (OPERATIONAL_RULES, DA_IDENTITY, PROJECTS) force a prune before growth; auto-generated files (ARCHITECTURE_SUMMARY, PRINCIPAL_TELOS) get more headroom because a regenerate can legitimately grow them. Raising a budget is a deliberate decision recorded in git, not a silent bump.
_convention: path is relative to ~/.claude ; maxBytes is the hard ceiling
_totalDoc: Hard ceiling on the SUM of all always-on files (per-file caps alone sum to 184K and never bound the whole). 100KB = the 7.0.0 target. Added 2026-07-11 (BPE P0, R3).

budgets.note: constitutional; tighten as duplication is removed
re-bloat target; low headroom on purpose
active-apps-only + PROJECTS_RETIRED.md split 2026-07-11 (23080->18164 B); hold the gain
the re-bloat engine; the proposal-inbox redirect will shrink this — then lower the budget
